spec_match.rationale: The page now targets the actual CSS Overflow text-overflow contract: ellipsis, clip, single-line overflow interaction, RTL/BiDi samples, and the line-clamp boundary. It no longer calls generic overflow/white-space/:focus support a feature probe.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the CSS-only field, JavaScript fallback field, select-active control, RTL sample mode, and field-width/readout surface. Each changed focus, selection, computed style, overflow metrics, fallback state, or direction in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still provides parser checks, live comparison, fallback pattern, and compatibility boundaries, now with clearer manual labeling for the visual clip-on-interaction behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces two-value text-overflow as an unsupported but non-blocking syntax, RTL/BiDi ellipsis placement samples, fallback-on/off behavior, and multi-line line-clamp as a separate boundary rather than a false pass.

references.rationale: The concept page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5146265241387008 and CSS Overflow 3 ellipsis interaction.

design_system.rationale: The rewritten page uses shared CSS variables, sets color whenever custom button backgrounds change, removes the stale raw-color critique, and keeps controls labeled.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP confirmed the lab reported a false native warning in Chrome 148 because it measured computed overflow, omitted the ChromeStatus link, and raised form-field issues. Post-fix testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v147/clip-text-overflow-on-user-interaction/compatibility-lab/ exercised CSS-only focus, fallback focus, select-active, RTL sample mode, and the line-clamp boundary. The page reported exact text-overflow parser support, two-value syntax as not required, focused CSSOM text-overflow values, overflow dimensions, selection length, fallback expansion, and manual visual-check wording. Console was clean and local page/style requests returned 200.
